steps.title: Factor the second equation to match the structure of the first
steps.content:
Starting with $\frac{cx}{4y} = 76$, I can factor this expression by separating the constant from the variable ratio:

$$\frac{cx}{4y} = \frac{c}{4} \cdot \frac{x}{y}$$

This factoring reveals that the second equation contains the same ratio $\frac{x}{y}$ that appears in the first equation.

steps.title: Substitute the known ratio and solve for c
steps.content:
From the first equation, I know that $\frac{x}{y} = 76$. Substituting this into the factored form of the second equation:

$$\frac{c}{4} \cdot 76 = 76$$

Dividing both sides by 76:

$$\frac{c}{4} = 1$$

Multiplying both sides by 4:

$$c = 4$$
